info.title: LLM Key Warning: 4xx Handling & Tests Adjustment

parts.text: There is a warning shown when no LLM key is set. A key isn't always needed. Verify that errors will be shown if an LLM provider returns a 4xx. If confirmed, remove the "Configure LLM Key in settings" warning. Ensure tests are updated.

22:     // a different origin (e.g. a local LM Studio / LiteLLM server).
23:     // The proxy is configured in vite.config.ts and rewrites /api/llm → <endpoint>.
24:     // In production, the VITE_LLM_USE_PROXY env var can be set to 'false' to
25:     // use the direct endpoint URL (when a proper reverse proxy is in place).
26:     const useProxy = import.meta.env.VITE_USE_PROXY !== 'false';
27:     if (useProxy) {
28:       return '/api/llm';
29:     }
30:     return this.config.llmEndpoint.replace(/\/$/, '');
31:   }
32: 
33:   private getHeaders(): Record<string, string> {
34:     const headers: Record<string, string> = {
35:       'Content-Type': 'application/json',
36:     };
37: 
38:     if (this.config.llmApiKey) {
39:       headers['Authorization'] = `Bearer ${this.config.llmApiKey}`;
40:     }
41: 
42:     return headers;
43:   }
44: 
45:   async chat(messages: LLMMessage[], options?: { signal?: AbortSignal }): Promise<string> {
46:     const request: LLMChatRequest = {
47:       model: this.config.llmModel,
48:       messages,
49:       stream: false,
50:     };
51: 
52:     const response = await fetch(`${this.getBaseUrl()}/chat/completions`, {
53:       method: 'POST',
54:       headers: this.getHeaders(),
55:       body: JSON.stringify(request),
56:       signal: options?.signal,
57:     });
58: 
59:     if (!response.ok) {
60:       const errorText = await response.text();
61:       throw new Error(`LLM API error ${response.status}: ${errorText}`);
62:     }
63: 
64:     const data = (await response.json()) as LLMChatResponse;
65:     return data.choices[0]?.message?.content ?? '';
66:   }
67: 
68:   async *chatStream(
69:     messages: LLMMessage[],
70:     options?: { signal?: AbortSignal }
71:   ): AsyncGenerator<string, void, unknown> {
72:     const request: LLMChatRequest = {
73:       model: this.config.llmModel,
74:       messages,
75:       stream: true,
76:     };
77: 
78:     const response = await fetch(`${this.getBaseUrl()}/chat/completions`, {
79:       method: 'POST',
80:       headers: this.getHeaders(),
81:       body: JSON.stringify(request),
82:       signal: options?.signal,
83:     });
84: 
85:     if (!response.ok) {
86:       const errorText = await response.text();
87:       throw new Error(`LLM API error ${response.status}: ${errorText}`);
88:     }
89: 
90:     if (!response.body) {
91:       throw new Error('No response body for streaming');
92:     }
93: 
94:     const reader = response.body.getReader();
95:     const decoder = new TextDecoder();
96:     let buffer = '';
97: 
98:     try {
99:       while (true) {
100:         const { done, value } = await reader.read();
101:         if (done) break;
102: 
103:         buffer += decoder.decode(value, { stream: true });
104:         const lines = buffer.split('\n');
105:         buffer = lines.pop() ?? '';
106: 
107:         for (const line of lines) {
108:           const trimmed = line.trim();
109:           if (!trimmed || trimmed === 'data: [DONE]') continue;
110: 
111:           if (trimmed.startsWith('data: ')) {
112:             const jsonStr = trimmed.slice(6);
113:             try {
114:               const chunk = JSON.parse(jsonStr) as LLMStreamChunk;
115:               const content = chunk.choices[0]?.delta?.content;
116:               if (content) {
117:                 yield content;
118:               }
119:             } catch {
120:               // Skip malformed JSON
121:             }
122:           }
123:         }
124:       }
125:     } finally {
126:       reader.releaseLock();
127:     }
128:   }
129: 
130:   buildSystemPrompt(context: PRContext): string {
131:     const { pr, files, comments, reviewComments, reviews } = context;
132: 
133:     const filesSummary = files
134:       .map(
135:         (f) =>
136:           `- ${f.filename} (${f.status}: +${f.additions}/-${f.deletions})`
137:       )
138:       .join('\n');
139: 
140:     const commentsContent = comments
141:       .map((c) => `**${c.user.login}**: ${c.body}`)
142:       .join('\n\n');
143: 
144:     const reviewsContent = reviews
145:       .map((r) => `**${r.user.login}** (${r.state}): ${r.body ?? '(no comment)'}`)
146:       .join('\n\n');
147: 
148:     const reviewCommentsContent = reviewComments
149:       .map(
150:         (rc) =>
151:           `**${rc.user.login}** on \`${rc.path}\`:\n> ${rc.diff_hunk}\n\n${rc.body}`
152:       )
153:       .join('\n\n');
154: 
155:     const commentsSection = comments.length > 0 ? `### PR Comments\n${commentsContent}` : '';
156:     const reviewsSection = reviews.length > 0 ? `### Reviews\n${reviewsContent}` : '';
157:     const inlineCommentsSection =
158:       reviewComments.length > 0 ? `### Inline Review Comments\n${reviewCommentsContent}` : '';
159: 
160:     const promptPreamble = `You are an expert code reviewer assistant helping to analyze a GitHub Pull Request.
161: 
162: ## Pull Request: #${pr.number} - ${pr.title}
163: 
164: **Author**: ${pr.user.login}
165: **State**: ${pr.state}${pr.merged ? ' (merged)' : ''}
166: **Branch**: \`${pr.head.ref}\` → \`${pr.base.ref}\`
167: **Stats**: +${pr.additions} additions, -${pr.deletions} deletions, ${pr.changed_files} files changed
168: 
169: ### Description
170: ${pr.body ?? '(no description)'}
171: 
172: ### Changed Files
173: ${filesSummary}
174: 
175: ### Diffs`;
176: 
177:     const promptPostamble = `${commentsSection ? `\n\n${commentsSection}` : ''}
178: 
179: ${reviewsSection ? `\n\n${reviewsSection}` : ''}
180: 
181: ${inlineCommentsSection ? `\n\n${inlineCommentsSection}` : ''}
182: 
183: ---
184: 
185: You have full context of this PR metadata and file summaries. Included diffs may be truncated by context budget. Answer questions about the code changes, provide code review feedback, explain what the changes do, identify potential issues, suggest improvements, and help the user understand the PR. Be specific and reference actual code from the included diff when relevant.`;
186: 
187:     const maxDiffBudget = Math.max(
188:       0,
189:       LLMService.MAX_SYSTEM_PROMPT_CHARS - promptPreamble.length - promptPostamble.length - 2
190:     );
191: 
192:     const diffSections = files
193:       .filter((f) => Boolean(f.patch))
194:       .map((f) => ({
195:         filename: f.filename,
196:         content: `### ${f.filename}\n\`\`\`diff\n${f.patch}\n\`\`\``,
197:       }));
198: 
199:     const includedDiffs: string[] = [];
200:     const omittedDiffFilenames: string[] = [];
201:     let usedDiffChars = 0;
202: 
203:     for (const diff of diffSections) {
204:       const separator = includedDiffs.length > 0 ? '\n\n' : '\n';
205:       const nextSize = usedDiffChars + separator.length + diff.content.length;
206: 
207:       if (nextSize <= maxDiffBudget) {
208:         includedDiffs.push(diff.content);
209:         usedDiffChars = nextSize;
210:       } else {
211:         omittedDiffFilenames.push(diff.filename);
212:       }
213:     }
214: 
215:     const omissionSummary =
216:       omittedDiffFilenames.length > 0
217:         ? `\n\n[Context budget] Omitted ${omittedDiffFilenames.length} diff(s): ${omittedDiffFilenames.join(', ')}`
218:         : '';
219: 
220:     const diffContent =
221:       includedDiffs.length > 0
222:         ? `\n${includedDiffs.join('\n\n')}${omissionSummary}`
223:         : `${omissionSummary || '\n(No textual diffs available)'}`;
224: 
225:     return `${promptPreamble}${diffContent}${promptPostamble}`;
226:   }
227: 
228:   buildSummaryPrompt(
229:     context: PRContext,
230:     summaryPrompt: string,
231:     summaryCommands: string
232:   ): string {
233:     const commitMessages = (context.commits ?? [])
234:       .map((commit: PRCommit) => `- ${commit.commit.message.split('\n')[0]}`)
235:       .join('\n');
236: 
237:     const diffFiles = context.files
238:       .filter((file) => Boolean(file.patch))
239:       .map((file) => `- ${file.filename}`)
240:       .join('\n');
241: 
242:     const commandsSection = summaryCommands.trim()
243:       ? `\nAdditional commands:\n${summaryCommands.trim()}`
244:       : '';
245: 
246:     return `${summaryPrompt.trim()}${commandsSection}
247: 
248: PR metadata:
249: - Number: #${context.pr.number}
250: - Title: ${context.pr.title}
251: - Author: ${context.pr.user.login}
252: - Branch: ${context.pr.head.ref} -> ${context.pr.base.ref}
253: - Diff stats: +${context.pr.additions}/-${context.pr.deletions}, ${context.pr.changed_files} files
254: 
255: PR description:
256: ${context.pr.body ?? '(no description)'}
257: 
258: Commit messages:
259: ${commitMessages || '(no commit messages available)'}
260: 
261: Changed files with textual diffs:
262: ${diffFiles || '(no textual diffs available)'}
263: 
264: Response contract (required):
265: - Start with an orientation section of exactly 2-4 lines.
266: - Add a "Focus Areas" section only when meaningful risk, complexity, or churn signals exist.
267: - Focus Areas count must stay within 0-4 items.
268: - If Focus Areas are present, each item must include:
269:   - where to review
270:   - why it matters
271:   - what to verify
272: - Keep language concise and reviewer-oriented.`;
273:   }
274: }
275: 
276: export function createLLMService(config: AppConfig): LLMService {
277:   return new LLMService(config);
278: }

(End of file - total 278 lines)
</content>

357:   function makeSSEStream(chunks: string[]): ReadableStream<Uint8Array> {
358:     const encoder = new TextEncoder();
359:     return new ReadableStream({
360:       start(controller) {
361:         for (const chunk of chunks) {
362:           controller.enqueue(encoder.encode(chunk));

<content>1: import { createSlice, PayloadAction } from '@reduxjs/toolkit';
2: import type { AppConfig } from '../../types';
3: 
4: const STORAGE_KEY = 'pr-review-chatbot-config';
5: 
6: export const DEFAULT_SUMMARY_PROMPT = `You are preparing a pull request review kickoff summary for an experienced engineer.
7: 
8: Use the provided PR title, description, commit messages, and textual diffs.
9: 
10: Write concise, concrete statements grounded in the actual changes.
11: 
12: Output format:
13: 1) Start with an orientation section that is exactly 2-4 short lines.
14: 2) If there are meaningful risk, complexity, or churn signals, add a "Focus Areas" section.
15: 3) Focus Areas are optional for simple PRs. If included, include 1-4 items only.
16: 4) Every focus area item must include:
17:    - where to review
18:    - why it matters
19:    - what to verify
20: 
21: Do not invent code that is not present in the context.`;
22: 
23: function getDefaultConfig(): AppConfig {
24:   return {
25:     githubPat: import.meta.env.VITE_GITHUB_PAT || '',
26:     llmApiKey: import.meta.env.VITE_LLM_API_KEY || '',
27:     githubInstance: import.meta.env.VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE || 'github.com',
28:     llmBackend: (import.meta.env.VITE_LLM_BACKEND as 'openai' | 'litellm') || 'openai',
29:     llmEndpoint: import.meta.env.VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT || 'https://api.openai.com/v1',
30:     llmModel: import.meta.env.VITE_LLM_MODEL || 'gpt-4o',
31:     demoMode: import.meta.env.VITE_DEMO_MODE === 'true',
32:     summaryEnabled: import.meta.env.VITE_SUMMARY_ENABLED !== 'false',
33:     summaryPrompt: DEFAULT_SUMMARY_PROMPT,
34:     summaryCommands: import.meta.env.VITE_SUMMARY_COMMANDS || '',
35:   };
36: }
37: 
38: // Sensitive fields that must NOT be persisted to localStorage.
39: // They are read from env vars on first load and must be re-entered each session.
40: const SENSITIVE_FIELDS: ReadonlyArray<keyof AppConfig> = ['githubPat', 'llmApiKey'];
41: 
42: function loadConfigFromStorage(): AppConfig {
43:   try {
44:     const stored = localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY);
45:     if (stored) {
46:       const parsed = JSON.parse(stored) as Partial<AppConfig>;
47:       // Never restore sensitive fields from storage — always fall back to env defaults
48:       SENSITIVE_FIELDS.forEach((key) => delete parsed[key]);
49:       return { ...getDefaultConfig(), ...parsed };
50:     }
51:   } catch {
52:     // ignore
53:   }
54:   return getDefaultConfig();
55: }
56: 
57: function saveConfigToStorage(config: AppConfig): void {
58:   try {
59:     // Strip sensitive fields before writing to localStorage
60:     const safeConfig = { ...config } as Partial<AppConfig>;
61:     SENSITIVE_FIELDS.forEach((key) => delete safeConfig[key]);
62:     localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(safeConfig));
63:   } catch {
64:     // ignore
65:   }
66: }
67: 
68: interface ConfigState {
69:   config: AppConfig;
70: }
71: 
72: const initialState: ConfigState = {
73:   config: loadConfigFromStorage(),
74: };
75: 
76: const configSlice = createSlice({
77:   name: 'config',
78:   initialState,
79:   reducers: {
80:     updateConfig(state, action: PayloadAction<Partial<AppConfig>>) {
81:       state.config = { ...state.config, ...action.payload };
82:       saveConfigToStorage(state.config);
83:     },
84:     resetConfig(state) {
85:       state.config = getDefaultConfig();
86:       saveConfigToStorage(state.config);
87:     },
88:     setDemoMode(state, action: PayloadAction<boolean>) {
89:       state.config.demoMode = action.payload;
90:       saveConfigToStorage(state.config);
91:     },
92:     resetSummaryPrompt(state) {
93:       state.config.summaryPrompt = DEFAULT_SUMMARY_PROMPT;
94:       saveConfigToStorage(state.config);
95:     },
96:   },
97: });
98: 
99: export const { updateConfig, resetConfig, setDemoMode, resetSummaryPrompt } = configSlice.actions;
100: export default configSlice.reducer;

<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { createSlice, PayloadAction } from '@reduxjs/toolkit';
2: import type { ChatMessage } from '../../types';
3: 
4: interface ChatState {
5:   messages: ChatMessage[];
6:   isStreaming: boolean;
7:   streamingMessageId: string | null;
8:   error: string | null;
9: }
10: 
11: const initialState: ChatState = {
12:   messages: [],
13:   isStreaming: false,
14:   streamingMessageId: null,
15:   error: null,
16: };
17: 
18: const chatSlice = createSlice({
19:   name: 'chat',
20:   initialState,
21:   reducers: {
22:     addMessage(state, action: PayloadAction<ChatMessage>) {
23:       state.messages.push(action.payload);
24:     },
25:     updateStreamingMessage(state, action: PayloadAction<{ id: string; content: string }>) {
26:       const message = state.messages.find((m) => m.id === action.payload.id);
27:       if (message) {
28:         message.content = action.payload.content;
29:       }
30:     },
31:     appendStreamingContent(state, action: PayloadAction<{ id: string; chunk: string }>) {
32:       const message = state.messages.find((m) => m.id === action.payload.id);
33:       if (message) {
34:         message.content += action.payload.chunk;
35:       }
36:     },
37:     finalizeStreamingMessage(state, action: PayloadAction<string>) {
38:       const message = state.messages.find((m) => m.id === action.payload);
39:       if (message) {
40:         message.isStreaming = false;
41:       }
42:       state.isStreaming = false;
43:       state.streamingMessageId = null;
44:     },
45:     setStreaming(state, action: PayloadAction<{ isStreaming: boolean; messageId?: string }>) {
46:       state.isStreaming = action.payload.isStreaming;
47:       state.streamingMessageId = action.payload.messageId ?? null;
48:     },
49:     setError(state, action: PayloadAction<string | null>) {
50:       state.error = action.payload;
51:       state.isStreaming = false;
52:       state.streamingMessageId = null;
53:     },
54:     clearMessages(state) {
55:       state.messages = [];
56:       state.isStreaming = false;
57:       state.streamingMessageId = null;
58:       state.error = null;
59:     },
60:     markMessageError(state, action: PayloadAction<{ id: string; error: string }>) {
61:       const message = state.messages.find((m) => m.id === action.payload.id);
62:       if (message) {
63:         message.isStreaming = false;
64:         message.error = action.payload.error;
65:       }
66:       state.isStreaming = false;
67:       state.streamingMessageId = null;
68:     },
69:   },
70: });
71: 
72: export const {
73:   addMessage,
74:   updateStreamingMessage,
75:   appendStreamingContent,
76:   finalizeStreamingMessage,
77:   setStreaming,
78:   setError,
79:   clearMessages,
80:   markMessageError,
81: } = chatSlice.actions;
82: 
83: export default chatSlice.reducer;

(End of file - total 83 lines)
</content>

7:   setStreaming,

9:   clearMessages,

11: } from './chatSlice';
12: import type { ChatMessage } from '../../types';
13: 
14: function makeMessage(overrides: Partial<ChatMessage> = {}): ChatMessage {
15:   return {
16:     id: 'msg-1',
17:     role: 'user',

20:     ...overrides,

22: }
23:

29: };
30: 
31: describe('chatSlice', () => {
32:   describe('addMessage', () => {
33:     it('appends a message to the list', () => {
34:       const msg = makeMessage();

40: 
41:   describe('updateStreamingMessage', () => {

43:       const state = {
44:         ...emptyState,
45:         messages: [makeMessage({ id: 'a', content: 'old' })],
46:       };
47:       const next = chatReducer(state, updateStreamingMessage({ id: 'a', content: 'new' }));
48:       expect(next.messages[0].content).toBe('new');
49:     });
50: 
51:     it('does nothing when id does not match', () => {
52:       const state = {
53:         ...emptyState,

55:       };
56:       const next = chatReducer(state, updateStreamingMessage({ id: 'z', content: 'new' }));
57:       expect(next.messages[0].content).toBe('old');
58:     });
59:   });
60: 
61:   describe('appendStreamingContent', () => {
62:     it('appends a chunk to the matching message content', () => {
63:       const state = {
64:         ...emptyState,
65:         messages: [makeMessage({ id: 'a', content: 'Hello' })],
66:       };
67:       const next = chatReducer(state, appendStreamingContent({ id: 'a', chunk: ' world' }));
68:       expect(next.messages[0].content).toBe('Hello world');
69:     });
70:   });
71: 
72:   describe('finalizeStreamingMessage', () => {
73:     it('sets isStreaming=false on the message and clears streaming state', () => {
74:       const state = {
75:         messages: [makeMessage({ id: 'a', isStreaming: true })],
76:         isStreaming: true,
77:         streamingMessageId: 'a',
78:         error: null,
79:       };
80:       const next = chatReducer(state, finalizeStreamingMessage('a'));
81:       expect(next.messages[0].isStreaming).toBe(false);

85:   });
86: 
87:   describe('setStreaming', () => {

96:       const next = chatReducer(state, setStreaming({ isStreaming: false }));

Add pagination (loop until `response.data.length < perPage`) or at minimum show a warning banner in `PRViewer` when the file count equals the page limit.

Either connect it to a `fetchPullRequests` thunk (part of Phase 7 GitHub UI work) or hide it with `{false && ...}` until that feature is ready, to avoid confusing users with a dead control.

<type>file</type>
<content>1: # PR Review Chatbot - Agent Instructions
2: 
3: ## Project Overview
4: 
5: This is a standalone React + TypeScript webapp that acts as a chatbot interface for reviewing and asking questions about GitHub Pull Requests. It uses an LLM (via OpenAI v1 API or LiteLLM Proxy) to answer questions about PR content.
6: 
7: ## Tech Stack
8: 
9: - **Vite** + **React 18** + **TypeScript**
10: - **Redux Toolkit** for state management
11: - **Tailwind CSS** for styling
12: - **Radix UI** for accessible components
13: - **Axios** for HTTP (GitHub API)
14: - **react-markdown** + **react-syntax-highlighter** for message rendering
15: 
16: ## Project Structure
17: 
18: ```
19: src/
20: ├── App.tsx                    # Root component with layout
21: ├── main.tsx                   # Entry point with Redux Provider
22: ├── index.css                  # Tailwind + CSS variables
23: ├── vite-env.d.ts              # Vite environment type declarations
24: ├── types/
25: │   └── index.ts               # All TypeScript interfaces
26: ├── store/
27: │   ├── index.ts               # Redux store configuration
28: │   ├── hooks.ts               # Typed useAppDispatch / useAppSelector
29: │   └── slices/
30: │       ├── configSlice.ts     # App config (env vars + localStorage overrides)
31: │       ├── chatSlice.ts       # Chat messages + streaming state
32: │       ├── prsSlice.ts        # Selected PR data + repo PR lists + commits + summary lifecycle state
33: │       └── watchedReposSlice.ts # Watched repositories + PR count badges (localStorage)
34: ├── services/
35: │   ├── llm.ts                 # LLM service (OpenAI v1 API + LiteLLM, streaming)
36: │   ├── github.ts              # GitHub API service (github.com + GHES)
37: │   ├── summary.ts             # Summary cache/rate-limit/empty-diff utilities
38: │   └── dummyData.ts           # Sample PR data for demo mode
39: ├── components/
40: │   ├── Sidebar.tsx            # Demo toggle + watchlist + controls/list sidebar views
41: │   ├── PRList.tsx             # Sidebar repository PR list view with back navigation
42: │   ├── ChatWindow.tsx         # Chat interface with streaming
43: │   ├── PRViewer.tsx           # PR details, summary tab, diffs, comments, reviews
44: │   └── SettingsDialog.tsx     # Runtime configuration override + summary controls
45: └── lib/
46:     └── utils.ts               # cn(), formatDate(), generateId()
47: ```
48: 
49: ## Key Features
50: 
51: ### Demo Mode (Default)
52: - Pre-loaded with a sample JWT authentication PR
53: - No GitHub credentials required
54: - Toggle in sidebar or Settings dialog
55: - Allows immediate LLM interaction
56: 
57: ### LLM Integration
58: - Supports **OpenAI v1 API** and **LiteLLM Proxy** (same API format)
59: - Streaming responses via Server-Sent Events
60: - PR context injected as system prompt (PR metadata, diffs, comments, reviews)
61: - Configurable endpoint, model, and API key
62: - Separate summary prompt assembly path (`buildSummaryPrompt`) for orientation-first PR kickoff summaries
63: 
64: ### GitHub Integration
65: - PAT (Personal Access Token) authentication
66: - Supports **github.com** and **GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES)**
67: - GHES base URL: `https://<host>/api/v3`
68: - Manual GitHub mode input in sidebar: `owner/repo` + optional PR number
69: - Sidebar supports `Load PR` (single selected list item) and `Load All PRs` (repository list)
70: - Watch repositories in sidebar and display open PR count badges
71: - Fetches: PR metadata, file diffs, issue comments, review comments, reviews, commit messages
72: - Refresh actions re-fetch currently selected PR, repository lists, and watched repo PR counts
73: - User-friendly GitHub API errors (auth, not found, rate limit, network)
74: 
75: ### Settings
76: - Runtime override of all configuration
77: - Persisted to `localStorage`
78: - Defaults from environment variables (`VITE_*`)
79: - Includes summary controls (`summaryEnabled`, editable `summaryPrompt`, optional `summaryCommands`, reset prompt)
80: 
81: ### PR Summary Flow
82: - Summary generation triggers on PR selection/load when `summaryEnabled` is true
83: - Summary state is isolated from chat state (`prsSlice.summary`)
84: - Fallback states:
85:   - `Nothing to Summarize` when no textual diffs exist
86:   - `Unable to generate summary` on generation failure
87: - Uses session-scoped cache and one-per-minute per-PR-head rate limit
88: - Summary output contract:
89:   - 2-4 line orientation section
90:   - adaptive `Focus Areas` (`0..4`)
91:   - each focus area includes where/why/what-to-verify
92: 
93: ## Environment Variables
94: 
95: ```bash
96: VITE_GITHUB_PAT=          # GitHub Personal Access Token
97: VITE_LLM_API_KEY=         # LLM API Key
98: VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=github.com  # "github.com" or GHES hostname
99: VITE_LLM_BACKEND=openai   # "openai" or "litellm"
100: VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1
101: VITE_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o
102: VITE_DEMO_MODE=true
103: VITE_SUMMARY_ENABLED=true
104: VITE_SUMMARY_COMMANDS=
105: ```
106: 
107: ## Development
108: 
109: ```bash
110: npm install       # Install dependencies
111: npm run dev       # Start dev server (http://localhost:5173)
112: npm run lint      # Run ESLint (flat config in eslint.config.js)
113: npm run build     # TypeScript check + production build
114: npm run preview   # Preview production build
115: npm run docker:build # Build Docker image locally
116: npm run docker:run   # Run Docker image locally on :8080
117: ```
118: 
119: ## CI/CD and Distribution
120: 
121: - CI workflow: `.github/workflows/build.yml`
122:   - Runs `npm ci`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` on pushes/PRs to `main`.
123: - Docker release workflow: `.github/workflows/docker-release.yml`
124:   - Triggers on GitHub Release `published`.
125:   - Publishes image to `ghcr.io/<owner>/<repo>`.
126:   - Computes image tags as semver-distance:
127:     - primary: `vX.Y.Z-N` (latest reachable semver tag + commit distance)
128:     - fallback: `v0.0.0-N` when no semver tags exist
129:     - traceability: `sha-<shortsha>`
130: - Docker runtime setup:
131:   - `Dockerfile` uses multi-stage Node build + nginx runtime.
132:   - `nginx.conf` serves SPA routes via `try_files ... /index.html`.
133:   - `.dockerignore` excludes local build, VCS, and env artifacts from build context.
134: 
135: ## Common Tasks for AI Agents
136: 
137: ### Adding a new Redux slice
138: 1. Create `src/store/slices/<name>Slice.ts` following the pattern in existing slices
139: 2. Add the reducer to `src/store/index.ts`
140: 3. Export typed selectors using `useAppSelector`
141: 
142: ### Adding a new component
143: 1. Create `src/components/<Name>.tsx`
144: 2. Use `useAppSelector` and `useAppDispatch` from `src/store/hooks.ts`
145: 3. Use `cn()` from `src/lib/utils.ts` for conditional class names
146: 4. Follow Tailwind CSS + CSS variable pattern for theming
147: 
148: ### Modifying the LLM system prompt
149: - Edit `buildSystemPrompt()` in [`src/services/llm.ts`](src/services/llm.ts)
150: - The method receives a `PRContext` object with `pr`, `files`, `comments`, `reviewComments`, `reviews`
151: - Prompt construction uses a deterministic character budget for very large PRs and emits an omission summary when some diffs are excluded
152: 
153: ### Modifying summary behavior
154: - Summary orchestration policy lives in [`src/services/summary.ts`](src/services/summary.ts)
155: - Summary state transitions and async generation thunk live in [`src/store/slices/prsSlice.ts`](src/store/slices/prsSlice.ts)
156: - Summary prompt contract lives in `buildSummaryPrompt()` in [`src/services/llm.ts`](src/services/llm.ts)
157: - Summary UI rendering lives in [`src/components/PRViewer.tsx`](src/components/PRViewer.tsx)
158: 
159: ### Adding GitHub API endpoints
160: - Add methods to the `GitHubService` class in [`src/services/github.ts`](src/services/github.ts)
161: - The service automatically handles github.com vs GHES URL routing
162: - Prefer using the centralized GitHub error parsing (`parseGitHubError`) and `GitHubApiError` for consistent UI-friendly failures
163: 
164: ### GitHub data loading flow
165: - `prsSlice` includes async thunks for PR metadata and per-resource loading:
166:   - `fetchPullRequest`
167:   - `fetchPRFiles`
168:   - `fetchPRComments`
169:   - `fetchPRReviewComments`
170:   - `fetchPRReviews`
171: - Use `loadingByResource` and `errorByResource` for UI state; legacy aggregate `isLoading` and `error` are still populated for compatibility
172: 
173: ### Active OpenSpec context
174: - Active change: `openspec/changes/repository-watcher`
175: - Main specs synced for this change:
176:   - `openspec/specs/repo-pr-list/spec.md`
177:   - `openspec/specs/watched-repos/spec.md`
178: - Repository watcher behavior target:
179:   - left `PR Review` pane supports two views: controls/watchlist and repo PR list
180:   - `Load PR` with a PR number switches to list view with one selected PR (demo-like behavior)
181:   - watched repo click opens that repo list in the same pane
182:   - list view includes Back action to return to controls/watchlist
183: 
184: ### Modifying dummy data
185: - Edit [`src/services/dummyData.ts`](src/services/dummyData.ts)
186: - The `dummyPRContext` export is used by `ChatWindow` and `Sidebar` in demo mode
187: 
188: ## Architecture Notes
189: 
190: ### State Flow
191: ```
192: User types message
193:   → ChatWindow dispatches addMessage (user)
194:   → ChatWindow dispatches addMessage (assistant, isStreaming: true)
195:   → LLMService.chatStream() yields chunks
196:   → ChatWindow dispatches appendStreamingContent for each chunk
197:   → ChatWindow dispatches finalizeStreamingMessage when done
198: ```
199: 
200: ### Config Priority
201: ```
202: localStorage override > environment variable > hardcoded default
203: ```
204: 
205: ### GHES URL Construction
206: ```typescript
207: // github.com → https://api.github.com
208: // mycompany.com → https://mycompany.com/api/v3
209: ```
210: 
211: ## Known Limitations / Future Work
212: 
213: - No pagination for PR lists
214: - No OAuth flow (PAT only)
215: - No conversation persistence across page reloads

(End of file - total 215 lines)
</content>

parts.text:
Confirmed by code inspection: 4xx errors are already surfaced to users, so removing the key warning is safe.

- `LLMService.chat()` and `LLMService.chatStream()` throw on any non-OK HTTP status (`src/services/llm.ts:59`, `src/services/llm.ts:85`), including 4xx.
- `ChatWindow` catches that error and stores the exact message on the assistant message via `markMessageError` (`src/components/ChatWindow.tsx:220`, `src/components/ChatWindow.tsx:227`).
- Error messages are rendered directly in the chat bubble (`src/components/ChatWindow.tsx:52`).
- There is already a test for 401 in non-stream chat (`src/services/llm.test.ts:266`) and non-OK stream handling exists (currently 500) (`src/services/llm.test.ts:418`), so behavior is consistent for 4xx.

Plan (no edits yet, since plan mode is active):

- Update `src/components/ChatWindow.tsx:367` to remove the `⚠️ Configure LLM API key in Settings` branch and show neutral runtime status when not in demo mode.
- Add/adjust tests to reflect the UX change:
  - `src/components/ChatWindow.test.tsx`: assert the warning text is no longer shown when `llmApiKey` is empty.
  - `src/components/ChatWindow.test.tsx`: add a regression test that a simulated `LLM API error 401` appears in the message bubble.
  - `src/services/llm.test.ts`: add an explicit `chatStream()` 4xx case (e.g., 401) to lock in expected behavior.
- After implementation, run validation: `npm run lint`, `npm run test`, and `npm run build`.
- Update `plans/plan.md` with this task and completion notes once edits are made.

If you want, I’ll execute this plan now.

# PR Review Chatbot - Agent Instructions

## Project Overview

This is a standalone React + TypeScript webapp that acts as a chatbot interface for reviewing and asking questions about GitHub Pull Requests. It uses an LLM (via OpenAI v1 API or LiteLLM Proxy) to answer questions about PR content.

## Tech Stack

- **Vite** + **React 18** + **TypeScript**
- **Redux Toolkit** for state management
- **Tailwind CSS** for styling
- **Radix UI** for accessible components
- **Axios** for HTTP (GitHub API)
- **react-markdown** + **react-syntax-highlighter** for message rendering

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── App.tsx                    # Root component with layout
├── main.tsx                   # Entry point with Redux Provider
├── index.css                  # Tailwind + CSS variables
├── vite-env.d.ts              # Vite environment type declarations
├── types/
│   └── index.ts               # All TypeScript interfaces
├── store/
│   ├── index.ts               # Redux store configuration
│   ├── hooks.ts               # Typed useAppDispatch / useAppSelector
│   └── slices/
│       ├── configSlice.ts     # App config (env vars + localStorage overrides)
│       ├── chatSlice.ts       # Chat messages + streaming state
│       ├── prsSlice.ts        # Selected PR data + repo PR lists + commits + summary lifecycle state
│       └── watchedReposSlice.ts # Watched repositories + PR count badges (localStorage)
├── services/
│   ├── llm.ts                 # LLM service (OpenAI v1 API + LiteLLM, streaming)
│   ├── github.ts              # GitHub API service (github.com + GHES)
│   ├── summary.ts             # Summary cache/rate-limit/empty-diff utilities
│   └── dummyData.ts           # Sample PR data for demo mode
├── components/
│   ├── Sidebar.tsx            # Demo toggle + watchlist + controls/list sidebar views
│   ├── PRList.tsx             # Sidebar repository PR list view with back navigation
│   ├── ChatWindow.tsx         # Chat interface with streaming
│   ├── PRViewer.tsx           # PR details, summary tab, diffs, comments, reviews
│   └── SettingsDialog.tsx     # Runtime configuration override + summary controls
└── lib/
    └── utils.ts               # cn(), formatDate(), generateId()
```

## Key Features

### Demo Mode (Default)
- Pre-loaded with a sample JWT authentication PR
- No GitHub credentials required
- Toggle in sidebar or Settings dialog
- Allows immediate LLM interaction

### LLM Integration
- Supports **OpenAI v1 API** and **LiteLLM Proxy** (same API format)
- Streaming responses via Server-Sent Events
- PR context injected as system prompt (PR metadata, diffs, comments, reviews)
- Configurable endpoint, model, and API key
- Separate summary prompt assembly path (`buildSummaryPrompt`) for orientation-first PR kickoff summaries

### GitHub Integration
- PAT (Personal Access Token) authentication
- Supports **github.com** and **GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES)**
- GHES base URL: `https://<host>/api/v3`
- Manual GitHub mode input in sidebar: `owner/repo` + optional PR number
- Sidebar supports `Load PR` (single selected list item) and `Load All PRs` (repository list)
- Watch repositories in sidebar and display open PR count badges
- Fetches: PR metadata, file diffs, issue comments, review comments, reviews, commit messages
- Refresh actions re-fetch currently selected PR, repository lists, and watched repo PR counts
- User-friendly GitHub API errors (auth, not found, rate limit, network)

### Settings
- Runtime override of all configuration
- Persisted to `localStorage`
- Defaults from environment variables (`VITE_*`)
- Includes summary controls (`summaryEnabled`, editable `summaryPrompt`, optional `summaryCommands`, reset prompt)

### PR Summary Flow
- Summary generation triggers on PR selection/load when `summaryEnabled` is true
- Summary state is isolated from chat state (`prsSlice.summary`)
- Fallback states:
  - `Nothing to Summarize` when no textual diffs exist
  - `Unable to generate summary` on generation failure
- Uses session-scoped cache and one-per-minute per-PR-head rate limit
- Summary output contract:
  - 2-4 line orientation section
  - adaptive `Focus Areas` (`0..4`)
  - each focus area includes where/why/what-to-verify

## Environment Variables

```bash
VITE_GITHUB_PAT=          # GitHub Personal Access Token
VITE_LLM_API_KEY=         # LLM API Key
VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=github.com  # "github.com" or GHES hostname
VITE_LLM_BACKEND=openai   # "openai" or "litellm"
VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1
VITE_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o
VITE_DEMO_MODE=true
VITE_SUMMARY_ENABLED=true
VITE_SUMMARY_COMMANDS=
```

## Development

```bash
npm install       # Install dependencies
npm run dev       # Start dev server (http://localhost:5173)
npm run lint      # Run ESLint (flat config in eslint.config.js)
npm run build     # TypeScript check + production build
npm run preview   # Preview production build
npm run docker:build # Build Docker image locally
npm run docker:run   # Run Docker image locally on :8080
```

## CI/CD and Distribution

- CI workflow: `.github/workflows/build.yml`
  - Runs `npm ci`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` on pushes/PRs to `main`.
- Docker release workflow: `.github/workflows/docker-release.yml`
  - Triggers on GitHub Release `published`.
  - Publishes image to `ghcr.io/<owner>/<repo>`.
  - Computes image tags as semver-distance:
    - primary: `vX.Y.Z-N` (latest reachable semver tag + commit distance)
    - fallback: `v0.0.0-N` when no semver tags exist
    - traceability: `sha-<shortsha>`
- Docker runtime setup:
  - `Dockerfile` uses multi-stage Node build + nginx runtime.
  - `nginx.conf` serves SPA routes via `try_files ... /index.html`.
  - `.dockerignore` excludes local build, VCS, and env artifacts from build context.

## Common Tasks for AI Agents

### Adding a new Redux slice
1. Create `src/store/slices/<name>Slice.ts` following the pattern in existing slices
2. Add the reducer to `src/store/index.ts`
3. Export typed selectors using `useAppSelector`

### Adding a new component
1. Create `src/components/<Name>.tsx`
2. Use `useAppSelector` and `useAppDispatch` from `src/store/hooks.ts`
3. Use `cn()` from `src/lib/utils.ts` for conditional class names
4. Follow Tailwind CSS + CSS variable pattern for theming

### Modifying the LLM system prompt
- Edit `buildSystemPrompt()` in [`src/services/llm.ts`](src/services/llm.ts)
- The method receives a `PRContext` object with `pr`, `files`, `comments`, `reviewComments`, `reviews`
- Prompt construction uses a deterministic character budget for very large PRs and emits an omission summary when some diffs are excluded

### Modifying summary behavior
- Summary orchestration policy lives in [`src/services/summary.ts`](src/services/summary.ts)
- Summary state transitions and async generation thunk live in [`src/store/slices/prsSlice.ts`](src/store/slices/prsSlice.ts)
- Summary prompt contract lives in `buildSummaryPrompt()` in [`src/services/llm.ts`](src/services/llm.ts)
- Summary UI rendering lives in [`src/components/PRViewer.tsx`](src/components/PRViewer.tsx)

### Adding GitHub API endpoints
- Add methods to the `GitHubService` class in [`src/services/github.ts`](src/services/github.ts)
- The service automatically handles github.com vs GHES URL routing
- Prefer using the centralized GitHub error parsing (`parseGitHubError`) and `GitHubApiError` for consistent UI-friendly failures

### GitHub data loading flow
- `prsSlice` includes async thunks for PR metadata and per-resource loading:
  - `fetchPullRequest`
  - `fetchPRFiles`
  - `fetchPRComments`
  - `fetchPRReviewComments`
  - `fetchPRReviews`
- Use `loadingByResource` and `errorByResource` for UI state; legacy aggregate `isLoading` and `error` are still populated for compatibility

### Active OpenSpec context
- Active change: `openspec/changes/repository-watcher`
- Main specs synced for this change:
  - `openspec/specs/repo-pr-list/spec.md`
  - `openspec/specs/watched-repos/spec.md`
- Repository watcher behavior target:
  - left `PR Review` pane supports two views: controls/watchlist and repo PR list
  - `Load PR` with a PR number switches to list view with one selected PR (demo-like behavior)
  - watched repo click opens that repo list in the same pane
  - list view includes Back action to return to controls/watchlist

### Modifying dummy data
- Edit [`src/services/dummyData.ts`](src/services/dummyData.ts)
- The `dummyPRContext` export is used by `ChatWindow` and `Sidebar` in demo mode

## Architecture Notes

### State Flow
```
User types message
  → ChatWindow dispatches addMessage (user)
  → ChatWindow dispatches addMessage (assistant, isStreaming: true)
  → LLMService.chatStream() yields chunks
  → ChatWindow dispatches appendStreamingContent for each chunk
  → ChatWindow dispatches finalizeStreamingMessage when done
```

### Config Priority
```
localStorage override > environment variable > hardcoded default
```

### GHES URL Construction
```typescript
// github.com → https://api.github.com
// mycompany.com → https://mycompany.com/api/v3
```

## Known Limitations / Future Work

- No pagination for PR lists
- No OAuth flow (PAT only)
- No conversation persistence across page reloads

# PR Review Chatbot - Agent Instructions

## Project Overview

This is a standalone React + TypeScript webapp that acts as a chatbot interface for reviewing and asking questions about GitHub Pull Requests. It uses an LLM (via OpenAI v1 API or LiteLLM Proxy) to answer questions about PR content.

## Tech Stack

- **Vite** + **React 18** + **TypeScript**
- **Redux Toolkit** for state management
- **Tailwind CSS** for styling
- **Radix UI** for accessible components
- **Axios** for HTTP (GitHub API)
- **react-markdown** + **react-syntax-highlighter** for message rendering

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── App.tsx                    # Root component with layout
├── main.tsx                   # Entry point with Redux Provider
├── index.css                  # Tailwind + CSS variables
├── vite-env.d.ts              # Vite environment type declarations
├── types/
│   └── index.ts               # All TypeScript interfaces
├── store/
│   ├── index.ts               # Redux store configuration
│   ├── hooks.ts               # Typed useAppDispatch / useAppSelector
│   └── slices/
│       ├── configSlice.ts     # App config (env vars + localStorage overrides)
│       ├── chatSlice.ts       # Chat messages + streaming state
│       ├── prsSlice.ts        # Selected PR data + repo PR lists + commits + summary lifecycle state
│       └── watchedReposSlice.ts # Watched repositories + PR count badges (localStorage)
├── services/
│   ├── llm.ts                 # LLM service (OpenAI v1 API + LiteLLM, streaming)
│   ├── github.ts              # GitHub API service (github.com + GHES)
│   ├── summary.ts             # Summary cache/rate-limit/empty-diff utilities
│   └── dummyData.ts           # Sample PR data for demo mode
├── components/
│   ├── Sidebar.tsx            # Demo toggle + watchlist + controls/list sidebar views
│   ├── PRList.tsx             # Sidebar repository PR list view with back navigation
│   ├── ChatWindow.tsx         # Chat interface with streaming
│   ├── PRViewer.tsx           # PR details, summary tab, diffs, comments, reviews
│   └── SettingsDialog.tsx     # Runtime configuration override + summary controls
└── lib/
    └── utils.ts               # cn(), formatDate(), generateId()
```

## Key Features

### Demo Mode (Default)
- Pre-loaded with a sample JWT authentication PR
- No GitHub credentials required
- Toggle in sidebar or Settings dialog
- Allows immediate LLM interaction

### LLM Integration
- Supports **OpenAI v1 API** and **LiteLLM Proxy** (same API format)
- Streaming responses via Server-Sent Events
- PR context injected as system prompt (PR metadata, diffs, comments, reviews)
- Configurable endpoint, model, and API key
- API key is optional for providers/endpoints that do not require auth
- HTTP API failures (including provider 4xx errors) are surfaced in assistant message bubbles
- Separate summary prompt assembly path (`buildSummaryPrompt`) for orientation-first PR kickoff summaries

### GitHub Integration
- PAT (Personal Access Token) authentication
- Supports **github.com** and **GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES)**
- GHES base URL: `https://<host>/api/v3`
- Manual GitHub mode input in sidebar: `owner/repo` + optional PR number
- Sidebar supports `Load PR` (single selected list item) and `Load All PRs` (repository list)
- Watch repositories in sidebar and display open PR count badges
- Fetches: PR metadata, file diffs, issue comments, review comments, reviews, commit messages
- Refresh actions re-fetch currently selected PR, repository lists, and watched repo PR counts
- User-friendly GitHub API errors (auth, not found, rate limit, network)

### Settings
- Runtime override of all configuration
- Persisted to `localStorage`
- Defaults from environment variables (`VITE_*`)
- Includes summary controls (`summaryEnabled`, editable `summaryPrompt`, optional `summaryCommands`, reset prompt)

### PR Summary Flow
- Summary generation triggers on PR selection/load when `summaryEnabled` is true
- Summary state is isolated from chat state (`prsSlice.summary`)
- Fallback states:
  - `Nothing to Summarize` when no textual diffs exist
  - `Unable to generate summary` on generation failure
- Uses session-scoped cache and one-per-minute per-PR-head rate limit
- Summary output contract:
  - 2-4 line orientation section
  - adaptive `Focus Areas` (`0..4`)
  - each focus area includes where/why/what-to-verify

## Environment Variables

```bash
VITE_GITHUB_PAT=          # GitHub Personal Access Token
VITE_LLM_API_KEY=         # LLM API Key
VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=github.com  # "github.com" or GHES hostname
VITE_LLM_BACKEND=openai   # "openai" or "litellm"
VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1
VITE_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o
VITE_DEMO_MODE=true
VITE_SUMMARY_ENABLED=true
VITE_SUMMARY_COMMANDS=
```

## Development

```bash
npm install       # Install dependencies
npm run dev       # Start dev server (http://localhost:5173)
npm run lint      # Run ESLint (flat config in eslint.config.js)
npm run build     # TypeScript check + production build
npm run preview   # Preview production build
npm run docker:build # Build Docker image locally
npm run docker:run   # Run Docker image locally on :8080
```

## CI/CD and Distribution

- CI workflow: `.github/workflows/build.yml`
  - Runs `npm ci`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` on pushes/PRs to `main`.
- Docker release workflow: `.github/workflows/docker-release.yml`
  - Triggers on GitHub Release `published`.
  - Publishes image to `ghcr.io/<owner>/<repo>`.
  - Computes image tags as semver-distance:
    - primary: `vX.Y.Z-N` (latest reachable semver tag + commit distance)
    - fallback: `v0.0.0-N` when no semver tags exist
    - traceability: `sha-<shortsha>`
- Docker runtime setup:
  - `Dockerfile` uses multi-stage Node build + nginx runtime.
  - `nginx.conf` serves SPA routes via `try_files ... /index.html`.
  - `.dockerignore` excludes local build, VCS, and env artifacts from build context.

## Common Tasks for AI Agents

### Adding a new Redux slice
1. Create `src/store/slices/<name>Slice.ts` following the pattern in existing slices
2. Add the reducer to `src/store/index.ts`
3. Export typed selectors using `useAppSelector`

### Adding a new component
1. Create `src/components/<Name>.tsx`
2. Use `useAppSelector` and `useAppDispatch` from `src/store/hooks.ts`
3. Use `cn()` from `src/lib/utils.ts` for conditional class names
4. Follow Tailwind CSS + CSS variable pattern for theming

### Modifying the LLM system prompt
- Edit `buildSystemPrompt()` in [`src/services/llm.ts`](src/services/llm.ts)
- The method receives a `PRContext` object with `pr`, `files`, `comments`, `reviewComments`, `reviews`
- Prompt construction uses a deterministic character budget for very large PRs and emits an omission summary when some diffs are excluded

### Modifying summary behavior
- Summary orchestration policy lives in [`src/services/summary.ts`](src/services/summary.ts)
- Summary state transitions and async generation thunk live in [`src/store/slices/prsSlice.ts`](src/store/slices/prsSlice.ts)
- Summary prompt contract lives in `buildSummaryPrompt()` in [`src/services/llm.ts`](src/services/llm.ts)
- Summary UI rendering lives in [`src/components/PRViewer.tsx`](src/components/PRViewer.tsx)

### Adding GitHub API endpoints
- Add methods to the `GitHubService` class in [`src/services/github.ts`](src/services/github.ts)
- The service automatically handles github.com vs GHES URL routing
- Prefer using the centralized GitHub error parsing (`parseGitHubError`) and `GitHubApiError` for consistent UI-friendly failures

### GitHub data loading flow
- `prsSlice` includes async thunks for PR metadata and per-resource loading:
  - `fetchPullRequest`
  - `fetchPRFiles`
  - `fetchPRComments`
  - `fetchPRReviewComments`
  - `fetchPRReviews`
- Use `loadingByResource` and `errorByResource` for UI state; legacy aggregate `isLoading` and `error` are still populated for compatibility

### Active OpenSpec context
- Active change: `openspec/changes/repository-watcher`
- Main specs synced for this change:
  - `openspec/specs/repo-pr-list/spec.md`
  - `openspec/specs/watched-repos/spec.md`
- Repository watcher behavior target:
  - left `PR Review` pane supports two views: controls/watchlist and repo PR list
  - `Load PR` with a PR number switches to list view with one selected PR (demo-like behavior)
  - watched repo click opens that repo list in the same pane
  - list view includes Back action to return to controls/watchlist

### Modifying dummy data
- Edit [`src/services/dummyData.ts`](src/services/dummyData.ts)
- The `dummyPRContext` export is used by `ChatWindow` and `Sidebar` in demo mode

## Architecture Notes

### State Flow
```
User types message
  → ChatWindow dispatches addMessage (user)
  → ChatWindow dispatches addMessage (assistant, isStreaming: true)
  → LLMService.chatStream() yields chunks
  → ChatWindow dispatches appendStreamingContent for each chunk
  → ChatWindow dispatches finalizeStreamingMessage when done
```

### Config Priority
```
localStorage override > environment variable > hardcoded default
```

### GHES URL Construction
```typescript
// github.com → https://api.github.com
// mycompany.com → https://mycompany.com/api/v3
```

## Known Limitations / Future Work

- No pagination for PR lists
- No OAuth flow (PAT only)
- No conversation persistence across page reloads

**Key Change**: Use dummy data for PR content to allow immediate interaction with the LLM without needing GitHub API credentials. GitHub integration can be added later.

## Configuration

```

### Runtime Override
Settings dialog allows overriding env vars at runtime (persisted to localStorage).

## Architecture

### Services
```
src/services/
├── github.ts      # Fetch PRs, details, diffs, comments (supports GHES)

```

### Redux Store
```
src/store/
├── index.ts              # Store configuration
├── hooks.ts              # Typed useAppDispatch / useAppSelector
└── slices/
    ├── configSlice.ts    # App config (env defaults + runtime overrides)
    ├── prsSlice.ts       # Current PR data
    └── chatSlice.ts      # Messages + streaming state
```

### Components
```
src/components/
├── SettingsDialog.tsx   # Runtime config override
├── Sidebar.tsx          # Repo/PR navigation (or demo mode toggle)
├── ChatWindow.tsx       # Chat messages + input
└── PRViewer.tsx         # File diffs, metadata
```

---

## Data Types

### GitHub PR
```typescript
interface PullRequest {
  id: number;
  number: number;
  title: string;
  body: string | null;
  state: 'open' | 'closed';
  merged: boolean;
  merged_at: string | null;
  user: GitHubUser;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
  head: { ref: string; sha: string; repo: { full_name: string } | null };
  base: { ref: string; sha: string; repo: { full_name: string } };
  url: string;
  html_url: string;
  diff_url: string;
  additions: number;
  deletions: number;
  changed_files: number;
  comments: number;
  review_comments: number;
  commits: number;
  labels: Array<{ id: number; name: string; color: string }>;
  requested_reviewers: GitHubUser[];
}
```

### Chat Message
```typescript
interface ChatMessage {
  id: string;
  role: 'user' | 'assistant' | 'system';
  content: string;
  timestamp: number;
  isStreaming?: boolean;
  error?: string;
}
```

### App Config
```typescript
interface AppConfig {
  githubPat: string;
  llmApiKey: string;
  githubInstance: string;
  llmBackend: 'openai' | 'litellm';
  llmEndpoint: string;
  llmModel: string;
  demoMode: boolean;
}
```

---

## Error Handling

### LLM API Errors
- Invalid API key: Error shown in message bubble
- Rate limiting: Error shown in message bubble
- Network errors: Error shown in message bubble
- Streaming errors: Graceful fallback with error message

### GitHub API Errors (when enabled)
- Invalid PAT: Prompt to update token
- Rate limiting: Show remaining requests
- Not found (404): Repository/PR not found

---

### ⏳ Phase 8: Code Quality & UX Improvements (PENDING)

Identified during initial code review. These are non-blocking improvements to address after the Phase 7 polish work.

#### Quality
- [x] **ESLint v9 flat config** (`eslint.config.js`) — Added repository-level flat config so `npm run lint` works with ESLint 9.
- [x] **`getPRFiles` pagination** (`src/services/github.ts:96`) — The GitHub API caps file lists at 100 per page. PRs with >100 changed files silently return incomplete data. Add pagination (loop until `response.data.length < perPage`) or at minimum show a warning banner in `PRViewer` when the file count equals the page limit.
- [x] **Stable React keys in `DiffView`** (`src/components/PRViewer.tsx:41`) — Diff lines currently use array index as `key`. Replace with a content-based key (e.g. `` `${i}-${line.slice(0, 8)}` ``) to prevent React reusing DOM nodes incorrectly when switching between files.

#### UX
- [x] **Persist dark mode preference** (`src/App.tsx:10`) — Dark mode state resets on every page reload. Initialise from `localStorage` and respect the OS `prefers-color-scheme` media query:
  ```ts
  const [darkMode, setDarkMode] = useState(
    () => localStorage.getItem('darkMode') === 'true'
      || window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches
  );
  ```
  Also persist the toggle: `localStorage.setItem('darkMode', String(!darkMode))` inside `toggleDarkMode`.
- [x] **Wire up the Refresh button** (`src/components/Sidebar.tsx:111`) — The refresh `<button>` in the sidebar is rendered in GitHub mode but has no `onClick` handler. Either connect it to a `fetchPullRequests` thunk (part of Phase 7 GitHub UI work) or hide it with `{false && ...}` until that feature is ready, to avoid confusing users with a dead control.

# PR Review Chatbot - Implementation Plan

## Priority: UI and LLM Integration First
**Key Change**: Use dummy data for PR content to allow immediate interaction with the LLM without needing GitHub API credentials. GitHub integration can be added later.

---

## Tech Stack
- Vite + React 18 + TypeScript
- Redux Toolkit for state management
- Axios for HTTP
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- Radix UI for accessible components

---

## Implementation Phases

### ✅ Phase 1: Project Setup (COMPLETED)
- [x] Initialize Vite + React + TypeScript project
- [x] Install dependencies (Redux Toolkit, Axios, Tailwind, Radix UI, react-markdown, react-syntax-highlighter, lucide-react)
- [x] Configure Tailwind CSS with CSS variables for theming
- [x] Set up project structure (`src/types`, `src/store`, `src/services`, `src/components`, `src/lib`)
- [x] Create `vite-env.d.ts` for Vite environment type declarations
- [x] Create `.env.example` with all configuration options
- [x] Create `.gitignore`

### ✅ Phase 2: UI Shell & Configuration (COMPLETED)
- [x] Create Redux store (`src/store/index.ts`)
- [x] Implement `configSlice.ts` — env var defaults + localStorage runtime overrides
- [x] Implement `chatSlice.ts` — messages, streaming state
- [x] Implement `prsSlice.ts` — selected PR, files, comments, reviews
- [x] Create typed Redux hooks (`src/store/hooks.ts`)
- [x] Create `SettingsDialog.tsx` — runtime config override with demo mode toggle
- [x] Create main `App.tsx` layout (sidebar + chat + PR viewer panels)
- [x] Dark mode toggle in top bar
- [x] Collapsible sidebar and PR viewer panels

### ✅ Phase 3: Chat Interface (COMPLETED)
- [x] Build `ChatWindow.tsx` with message display
- [x] User/assistant message bubbles with avatars
- [x] Markdown rendering with `react-markdown` + `remark-gfm`
- [x] Code syntax highlighting with `react-syntax-highlighter`
- [x] Streaming response display (animated cursor)
- [x] Auto-scroll to latest message
- [x] Multi-line textarea input (auto-resize)
- [x] Enter to send, Shift+Enter for new line
- [x] Suggestion chips on empty state
- [x] Clear conversation button
- [x] Error display in message bubbles

### ✅ Phase 4: LLM Integration (COMPLETED)
- [x] Implement `LLMService` class in `src/services/llm.ts`
- [x] OpenAI v1 API support (`/chat/completions`)
- [x] LiteLLM Proxy support (same API format)
- [x] Streaming via Server-Sent Events (SSE)
- [x] `buildSystemPrompt()` — injects full PR context (metadata, diffs, comments, reviews)
- [x] Connect LLM service to `ChatWindow` with streaming dispatch
- [x] Error handling for API failures

### ✅ Phase 5: Dummy Data & Demo Mode (COMPLETED)
- [x] Create `src/services/dummyData.ts` with realistic sample PR
  - Sample PR: JWT authentication feature (8 files, realistic diffs)
  - Sample comments, review comments, and reviews
- [x] Build `PRViewer.tsx` with:
  - PR metadata header (state badge, author, branch, stats)
  - Tabs: Files, Comments, Reviews
  - Collapsible file items with diff view
  - Diff syntax highlighting (green/red/blue lines)
  - Status badges per file (added/modified/removed)
- [x] Build `Sidebar.tsx` with demo mode toggle
- [x] Auto-load dummy PR on startup in demo mode
- [x] Demo mode toggle in sidebar and Settings dialog

### ✅ Phase 6: GitHub Integration (COMPLETED)
- [x] Implement `GitHubService` class in `src/services/github.ts`
- [x] PAT (Bearer token) authentication
- [x] Support for github.com (`https://api.github.com`)
- [x] Support for GHES (`https://<host>/api/v3`)
- [x] Methods: `listRepositories`, `searchRepositories`, `listPullRequests`, `getPullRequest`, `getPRFiles`, `getPRComments`, `getPRReviewComments`, `getPRReviews`, `validateToken`
- [x] Token validation method

### ✅ Phase 7: Polish (COMPLETED)
- [x] Add loading states and loading indicators for GitHub API calls
- [x] Add error handling for GitHub API failures (rate limiting, 404, auth errors)
- [x] Polish UI/UX (animations, transitions)
- [x] GitHub mode UI — allow entering repo/PR manually
- [x] Context window management for very large PRs

---

## Documentation (COMPLETED)
- [x] `README.md` — User-facing docs: quick start, configuration, LLM setup, GitHub setup, troubleshooting
- [x] `agents.md` — Developer/agent docs: project structure, architecture, common tasks
- [x] `.env.example` — Annotated environment variable reference

---

## Configuration

### Environment Variables (with defaults)
```
VITE_GITHUB_PAT=          # GitHub Personal Access Token
VITE_LLM_API_KEY=         # LLM API Key
VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=     # "github.com" or URL for GHES
VITE_LLM_BACKEND=         # "openai" or "litellm"
VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=        # API endpoint URL
VITE_LLM_MODEL=           # Model name
VITE_DEMO_MODE=           # "true" or "false"
```

### Runtime Override
Settings dialog allows overriding env vars at runtime (persisted to localStorage).

---

## Architecture

### Services
```
src/services/
├── github.ts      # Fetch PRs, details, diffs, comments (supports GHES)
├── llm.ts         # Unified interface for OpenAI v1 API + LiteLLM Proxy
└── dummyData.ts   # Sample PR data for demo mode
```

### Redux Store
```
src/store/
├── index.ts              # Store configuration
├── hooks.ts              # Typed useAppDispatch / useAppSelector
└── slices/
    ├── configSlice.ts    # App config (env defaults + runtime overrides)
    ├── prsSlice.ts       # Current PR data
    └── chatSlice.ts      # Messages + streaming state
```

### Components
```
src/components/
├── SettingsDialog.tsx   # Runtime config override
├── Sidebar.tsx          # Repo/PR navigation (or demo mode toggle)
├── ChatWindow.tsx       # Chat messages + input
└── PRViewer.tsx         # File diffs, metadata
```

---

## Data Types

### GitHub PR
```typescript
interface PullRequest {
  id: number;
  number: number;
  title: string;
  body: string | null;
  state: 'open' | 'closed';
  merged: boolean;
  merged_at: string | null;
  user: GitHubUser;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
  head: { ref: string; sha: string; repo: { full_name: string } | null };
  base: { ref: string; sha: string; repo: { full_name: string } };
  url: string;
  html_url: string;
  diff_url: string;
  additions: number;
  deletions: number;
  changed_files: number;
  comments: number;
  review_comments: number;
  commits: number;
  labels: Array<{ id: number; name: string; color: string }>;
  requested_reviewers: GitHubUser[];
}
```

### Chat Message
```typescript
interface ChatMessage {
  id: string;
  role: 'user' | 'assistant' | 'system';
  content: string;
  timestamp: number;
  isStreaming?: boolean;
  error?: string;
}
```

### App Config
```typescript
interface AppConfig {
  githubPat: string;
  llmApiKey: string;
  githubInstance: string;
  llmBackend: 'openai' | 'litellm';
  llmEndpoint: string;
  llmModel: string;
  demoMode: boolean;
}
```

---

## Error Handling

### LLM API Errors
- Invalid API key: Error shown in message bubble
- Rate limiting: Error shown in message bubble
- Network errors: Error shown in message bubble
- Streaming errors: Graceful fallback with error message

### GitHub API Errors (when enabled)
- Invalid PAT: Prompt to update token
- Rate limiting: Show remaining requests
- Not found (404): Repository/PR not found

---

### ⏳ Phase 8: Code Quality & UX Improvements (PENDING)

Identified during initial code review. These are non-blocking improvements to address after the Phase 7 polish work.

#### Quality
- [x] **ESLint v9 flat config** (`eslint.config.js`) — Added repository-level flat config so `npm run lint` works with ESLint 9.
- [x] **`getPRFiles` pagination** (`src/services/github.ts:96`) — The GitHub API caps file lists at 100 per page. PRs with >100 changed files silently return incomplete data. Add pagination (loop until `response.data.length < perPage`) or at minimum show a warning banner in `PRViewer` when the file count equals the page limit.
- [x] **Stable React keys in `DiffView`** (`src/components/PRViewer.tsx:41`) — Diff lines currently use array index as `key`. Replace with a content-based key (e.g. `` `${i}-${line.slice(0, 8)}` ``) to prevent React reusing DOM nodes incorrectly when switching between files.

#### UX
- [x] **Persist dark mode preference** (`src/App.tsx:10`) — Dark mode state resets on every page reload. Initialise from `localStorage` and respect the OS `prefers-color-scheme` media query:
  ```ts
  const [darkMode, setDarkMode] = useState(
    () => localStorage.getItem('darkMode') === 'true'
      || window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches
  );
  ```
  Also persist the toggle: `localStorage.setItem('darkMode', String(!darkMode))` inside `toggleDarkMode`.
- [x] **Wire up the Refresh button** (`src/components/Sidebar.tsx:111`) — The refresh `<button>` in the sidebar is rendered in GitHub mode but has no `onClick` handler. Either connect it to a `fetchPullRequests` thunk (part of Phase 7 GitHub UI work) or hide it with `{false && ...}` until that feature is ready, to avoid confusing users with a dead control.

---

## OpenSpec Alignment Notes (2026-03-14)

- `openspec/changes/add-pr-summary-tab/tasks.md` was updated to capture quick-orientation behavior decisions from explore mode.
- Summary behavior target: 2-4 line orientation section, adaptive `Focus Areas` with bounds `0..4`, and valid success output when focus areas are omitted for simple PRs.
- Synced delta spec `openspec/changes/add-pr-summary-tab/specs/pr-review-summary/spec.md` into new main spec `openspec/specs/pr-review-summary/spec.md`.
- Added change scaffold `openspec/changes/update-pr-review-summary-ui-readability/` with completed proposal, design, spec delta, and tasks artifacts for summary UI readability improvements.
- Refined `update-pr-review-summary-ui-readability` scope to panelized Summary display: Orientation and each Focus Area render as separate cards styled like Comments/Reviews, with blue/yellow title accents and no spec contract changes.

---

## ✅ Phase 9: PR Summary Tab (COMPLETED)

- [x] Extend `AppConfig` with summary fields: `summaryEnabled`, `summaryPrompt`, `summaryCommands`
- [x] Add/export `DEFAULT_SUMMARY_PROMPT` and keep reset behavior prompt-only
- [x] Add PR commits API loading (`getPRCommits`) and Redux state wiring
- [x] Add summary lifecycle state in `prsSlice` (`status`, `content`, `generatedAt`, `error`)
- [x] Implement summary orchestration utilities:
  - per-PR+head+prompt cache keying
  - sessionStorage read/write cache
  - one-per-minute per-PR-head rate limiting
  - empty textual diff detection
- [x] Extend LLM service with structured summary prompt assembly (orientation + adaptive focus areas)
- [x] Add Summary tab UI in `PRViewer` with loading/success/empty/error states and timestamp footer
- [x] Wire summary generation on PR selection/load and keep summary separate from chat history/context
- [x] Add/expand tests for config, GitHub service, PR slice, summary utilities, LLM prompt, Settings UI, and Summary tab rendering
- [x] Update README and agent guidance for summary behavior, fallback states, and output contract

---

## OpenSpec Implementation Updates (2026-03-14)

- [x] Completed OpenSpec change `update-pr-review-summary-ui-readability` implementation tasks (8/8) for panelized Summary tab readability updates.
- [x] Updated Summary success rendering in `src/components/PRViewer.tsx` to show an `Orientation` panel and per-item `Focus Area N` panels with blue/yellow title accents.
- [x] Kept summary generation flow and fallback state semantics unchanged (loading/empty/error behavior preserved).
- [x] Updated `src/components/PRViewer.summary.test.tsx` assertions for orientation-only and orientation+focus-area panelized rendering.
- [x] Validation run completed with `npm run lint && npm run build && npm run test`.

## OpenSpec Explore Decision Updates (2026-03-16)

- [x] Clarified `repository-watcher` UX scope: `repo-pr-list` renders in the left `PR Review` pane (not center chat area or right PR viewer pane).
- [x] Captured sidebar navigator behavior: controls/watchlist view and repo list view with explicit Back action.
- [x] Captured `Load PR` behavior parity with demo mode: when PR number is provided, show a list containing one selected PR.
- [x] Updated change artifacts (`proposal.md`, `design.md`, `tasks.md`, `specs/repo-pr-list/spec.md`, `specs/watched-repos/spec.md`) to reflect the decision.

## OpenSpec Implementation Updates (2026-03-16)

- [x] Implemented `repository-watcher` change tasks end-to-end.
- [x] Added watched repository state and persistence via `src/store/slices/watchedReposSlice.ts`.
- [x] Added repository PR list loading in `src/store/slices/prsSlice.ts` using existing `listPullRequests()` integration.
- [x] Added sidebar repo-list UI mode with Back navigation and new `src/components/PRList.tsx` component.
- [x] Updated sidebar to support optional PR number flow (`Load PR` vs `Load All PRs`) and watchlist interactions.
- [x] Added tests for watched repos, repository PR list loading, and sidebar list selection flows.
- [x] Validation run completed with `npm run lint && npm run build && npm run test`.

## OpenSpec Spec Sync Updates (2026-03-16)

- [x] Synced change `repository-watcher` delta specs into new main specs for `repo-pr-list` and `watched-repos`.
- [x] Created `openspec/specs/repo-pr-list/spec.md` from `openspec/changes/repository-watcher/specs/repo-pr-list/spec.md`.
- [x] Created `openspec/specs/watched-repos/spec.md` from `openspec/changes/repository-watcher/specs/watched-repos/spec.md`.

---

## Code Review Hardening Tasks (2026-03-17)

- [x] Task 1: Add stale-response guards in `prsSlice` so older PR resource responses cannot overwrite newer selection state.
- [x] Task 2: Add regression tests for rapid PR switching race in `src/store/slices/prsSlice.test.ts`.
- [x] Task 3: Add `Dialog.Description` in `src/components/SettingsDialog.tsx` to satisfy Radix dialog accessibility contract.
- [x] Task 4: Update settings dialog tests to assert description semantics and eliminate warning output.
- [x] Task 5: Add chat stream request cancellation (`AbortController`) in `src/components/ChatWindow.tsx` and `src/services/llm.ts`.
- [x] Task 6: Add tests for stream cancellation behavior and UI streaming state cleanup.

## Distribution and Release Automation (2026-03-17)

- [x] Added multi-stage Docker image build with static asset runtime (`Dockerfile`) and SPA-safe nginx config (`nginx.conf`).
- [x] Added `.dockerignore` and npm helper scripts (`docker:build`, `docker:run`) for local image usage.
- [x] Added release-triggered GHCR publish workflow (`.github/workflows/docker-release.yml`) with semver-distance tagging (`vX.Y.Z-N`) and fallback (`v0.0.0-N`).

## LLM Key Warning Cleanup (2026-03-18)

- [x] `agents.md` — Developer/agent docs: project structure, architecture, common tasks

---

## Configuration

### Environment Variables (with defaults)
```
VITE_GITHUB_PAT=          # GitHub Personal Access Token
VITE_LLM_API_KEY=         # LLM API Key
VITE_GITHUB_INSTANCE=     # "github.com" or URL for GHES
VITE_LLM_BACKEND=         # "openai" or "litellm"
VITE_LLM_ENDPOINT=        # API endpoint URL
VITE_LLM_MODEL=           # Model name
VITE_DEMO_MODE=           # "true" or "false"
```

### Runtime Override
Settings dialog allows overriding env vars at runtime (persisted to localStorage).

---

## Architecture

### Services
```
src/services/
├── github.ts      # Fetch PRs, details, diffs, comments (supports GHES)
├── llm.ts         # Unified interface for OpenAI v1 API + LiteLLM Proxy
└── dummyData.ts   # Sample PR data for demo mode
```

### Redux Store
```
src/store/
├── index.ts              # Store configuration
├── hooks.ts              # Typed useAppDispatch / useAppSelector
└── slices/
    ├── configSlice.ts    # App config (env defaults + runtime overrides)
    ├── prsSlice.ts       # Current PR data
    └── chatSlice.ts      # Messages + streaming state
```

### Components
```
src/components/
├── SettingsDialog.tsx   # Runtime config override
├── Sidebar.tsx          # Repo/PR navigation (or demo mode toggle)
├── ChatWindow.tsx       # Chat messages + input
└── PRViewer.tsx         # File diffs, metadata
```

---

## Data Types

### GitHub PR
```typescript
interface PullRequest {
  id: number;
  number: number;
  title: string;
  body: string | null;
  state: 'open' | 'closed';
  merged: boolean;
  merged_at: string | null;
  user: GitHubUser;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
  head: { ref: string; sha: string; repo: { full_name: string } | null };
  base: { ref: string; sha: string; repo: { full_name: string } };
  url: string;
  html_url: string;
  diff_url: string;
  additions: number;
  deletions: number;
  changed_files: number;
  comments: number;
  review_comments: number;
  commits: number;
  labels: Array<{ id: number; name: string; color: string }>;
  requested_reviewers: GitHubUser[];
}
```

### Chat Message
```typescript
interface ChatMessage {
  id: string;
  role: 'user' | 'assistant' | 'system';
  content: string;
  timestamp: number;
  isStreaming?: boolean;
  error?: string;
}
```

### App Config
```typescript
interface AppConfig {
  githubPat: string;
  llmApiKey: string;
  githubInstance: string;
  llmBackend: 'openai' | 'litellm';
  llmEndpoint: string;
  llmModel: string;
  demoMode: boolean;
}
```

---

## Error Handling

### LLM API Errors
- Invalid API key: Error shown in message bubble
- Rate limiting: Error shown in message bubble
- Network errors: Error shown in message bubble
- Streaming errors: Graceful fallback with error message

### GitHub API Errors (when enabled)
- Invalid PAT: Prompt to update token
- Rate limiting: Show remaining requests
- Not found (404): Repository/PR not found

---

### ⏳ Phase 8: Code Quality & UX Improvements (PENDING)

Identified during initial code review. These are non-blocking improvements to address after the Phase 7 polish work.

#### Quality
- [x] **ESLint v9 flat config** (`eslint.config.js`) — Added repository-level flat config so `npm run lint` works with ESLint 9.
- [x] **`getPRFiles` pagination** (`src/services/github.ts:96`) — The GitHub API caps file lists at 100 per page. PRs with >100 changed files silently return incomplete data. Add pagination (loop until `response.data.length < perPage`) or at minimum show a warning banner in `PRViewer` when the file count equals the page limit.
- [x] **Stable React keys in `DiffView`** (`src/components/PRViewer.tsx:41`) — Diff lines currently use array index as `key`. Replace with a content-based key (e.g. `` `${i}-${line.slice(0, 8)}` ``) to prevent React reusing DOM nodes incorrectly when switching between files.

#### UX
- [x] **Persist dark mode preference** (`src/App.tsx:10`) — Dark mode state resets on every page reload. Initialise from `localStorage` and respect the OS `prefers-color-scheme` media query:
  ```ts
  const [darkMode, setDarkMode] = useState(
    () => localStorage.getItem('darkMode') === 'true'
      || window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches
  );
  ```
  Also persist the toggle: `localStorage.setItem('darkMode', String(!darkMode))` inside `toggleDarkMode`.
- [x] **Wire up the Refresh button** (`src/components/Sidebar.tsx:111`) — The refresh `<button>` in the sidebar is rendered in GitHub mode but has no `onClick` handler. Either connect it to a `fetchPullRequests` thunk (part of Phase 7 GitHub UI work) or hide it with `{false && ...}` until that feature is ready, to avoid confusing users with a dead control.

---
